name: Torrie Zito
description: American pianist, music arranger, composer and conductor (1933-2009)
abstract: Salvatore "Torrie" Zito (October 12, 1933 – December 3, 2009) was an American pianist, music arranger, composer and conductor.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: by the time I was 16 I met Torrie and Ronnie Zito... I’d go to visit Ronnie and Ronnie and I would lie on the floor in front of the phonograph and listen to all these records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: Who walks out to conduct us is Torrie Zito. I go well this is poo man. So we start to do the rehearsal and Torrie stops the orchestra...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Caramanica
about_quotes.evidence: And from my era... there was a guy named Torrie Zito of the Zito brothers... Torrie Zito was a famous arranger, also a Utica guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Caramanica interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, July 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And I always loved the dates with Tony Bennett. Johnny Mandel came in, Roy Burns, Torrie Zito, great conductors, great bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
